overall.description: First-person cockpit view from a space combat simulation game. The player navigates through a narrow asteroid field while targeting a distant object, then emerges into open space revealing a large planet. Features futuristic HUD elements with orange holographic displays and high-fidelity space environments.

scene_state.functional_zone: narrow asteroid canyon with artificial lighting strips

events.description: target lock is lost and HUD displays NO TARGET warning

agents.ego_description: piloting spacecraft through narrow rocky canyon, initially engaging a distant target before losing lock at 5 seconds

large rocky asteroid wall with rough surface texture
horizontal glowing light strips mounted on the rock walls

segments.scene_description: Navigating through a narrow asteroid canyon with rocky walls passing by on both sides. Artificial light strips illuminate the path ahead while the spacecraft maintains a targeting lock that is lost at 5 seconds. The environment is dark and enclosed with the ship flying steadily forward.

scene_state.functional_zone: open space with large planetary body

events.description: spacecraft exits the asteroid field revealing a large planet dominating the viewport

agents.ego_description: flying through open space toward a large planet, maintaining steady ascent as the planetary surface details become visible

entered.detail: massive planetary body with visible atmosphere and surface details
faint atmospheric haze surrounding the planet's edge

segments.scene_description: The spacecraft emerges from the asteroid canyon into vast open space, revealing a massive planet that fills the forward view. The ship continues its ascent toward the planet, with atmospheric glow and surface details becoming more prominent as the rocky formations recede behind.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person flight from the claustrophobic depths of an asteroid canyon into the vast expanse of open space. It begins by navigating a narrow rocky corridor where artificial light strips illuminate the dark, enclosed walls passing closely on both sides. As the spacecraft maintains its steady forward momentum, the targeting lock on the HUD flickers and vanishes just as the rocky formations begin to recede. The scene then opens abruptly as the ship emerges from the canyon, shifting from the dim shadows of the asteroids to the brilliant glow of a massive planet that fills the forward view. Finally, the camera ascends toward the planetary body, with the atmospheric haze and surface details becoming increasingly prominent against the void.
